NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent, cross-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to generate new skills from experience, curate long-term memory, and build user models across sessions. The agent integrates with 14 messaging platforms and supports six execution backends, ranging from local Docker environments to serverless cloud infrastructure. Users can switch between various LLM providers and models via a unified CLI or messaging interface without changing code.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement from task experience
Multi-platform support including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and Signal
Flexible execution across local, SSH, Docker, and serverless backends

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.
highlights: Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter.summary: The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that allows Hermes Agent to function as a managed employee within the Paperclip company ecosystem. It works by wrapping the Hermes CLI to parse raw terminal output into structured transcript entries, enabling Paperclip to render interactive tool cards and status icons. The adapter manages session persistence across heartbeats, allowing agents to maintain context while responding to task assignments or issue comments. It also synchronizes skills between local directories and the Paperclip interface while reclassifying system logs to ensure a clean user experience.
highlights: Integrates 30+ native tools and 80+ skills into Paperclip
Parses raw CLI output into structured markdown and tool cards
Supports eight inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.
highlights: Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch of multiple persistent agents.
Zero-fork integration with vanilla NousResearch Hermes Agent installations.

yepyhun/Brainstack.summary: Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to provide Hermes agents with a structured memory model rather than a flat data blob. It integrates three distinct memory lines—Hindsight, Graphiti, and MemPalace—to manage session continuity, temporal graph relations, and large-corpus retrieval through a single memory owner. The system processes queries by routing them to specific "shelves" such as profile, graph, or corpus, ensuring Hermes receives a compact, purpose-built evidence packet. This approach prevents prompt stuffing and maintains temporal truth by separating historical facts from recent session context.
highlights: Consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into one kernel.
Uses a five-shelf architecture to prevent flat memory blob issues.
Integrates directly as a native MemoryProvider plugin for Hermes agents.

alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer.summary: Hermes Tool Slimmer is a context-optimization plugin that reduces prompt overhead by dynamically filtering large tool catalogs into a smaller, relevant subset. It builds an indexable corpus from Hermes tool schemas and ranks them using local BM25 keyword selection and explicit boosts. This process can significantly decrease the token footprint of requests in environments with dozens of native or MCP tools. The project includes a dashboard for monitoring estimated token savings and provides a fail-safe mechanism that reverts to the full schema list if errors occur.
highlights: Reduces schema overhead using BM25 keyword ranking and explicit boosts.
Supports Anthropic Tool Search and deferred tool loading modes.
Includes a dashboard plugin for tracking estimated token savings.

BORT-AGENTS/hermes-bort.summary: Hermes-bort is a plugin for Hermes Agent that enables interaction with BORT agent NFTs on the Binance Smart Chain mainnet. It allows Hermes processes to read agent states, execute on-chain actions via operator-signed writes, and manage portable session memory. The plugin integrates with the BORT logic contracts and knowledge registries to anchor chat history and evolution metrics to IPFS. This setup facilitates a persistent identity and memory for autonomous agents that can be transferred alongside the underlying NFT.
highlights: Reads multi-source agent state, logic snapshots, and marketplace data.
Executes operator-signed writes gated by local policy and approval prompts.
Anchors session memory to IPFS and on-chain knowledge registries.
